info.title: Azimut.ai API
info.description:
Customer-facing HTTP REST API for integrating Azimut's vessel-tracking data
into your platforms.

The **Pull API** documented here lets you query confirmed vessel transits
through your monitored areas, drill into the underlying image sightings,
and look up vessel identities.

* **Authentication.** OAuth 2.0 client_credentials. Mint a Bearer JWT and
  pass it as `Authorization: Bearer …`.
* **Errors.** RFC 7807 `application/problem+json` on every non-2xx response,
  with a stable machine-readable `code`.
* **Pagination.** Opaque cursor on every list endpoint.
* **Environments.** Production and sandbox use identical contracts; only
  the base URL differs.

See the [Getting Started](getting-started) guide for an end-to-end walkthrough.

get.description:
Cheap liveness check — never hits upstreams.

Wired to the chart's `livenessProbe`. Must stay dependency-free so an
upstream outage cannot cascade-restart every gateway replica.

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source: curl -X GET "http://localhost:8080/v1/health"

x-codeSamples.label: Python (httpx)
x-codeSamples.source:
import httpx

response = httpx.get("http://localhost:8080/v1/health")
response.raise_for_status()
data = response.json()

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (fetch)

get.description:
Readiness check — exercises the upstreams the gateway needs.

Wired to the chart's `readinessProbe`. Failure here removes the pod
from the Service (does NOT restart it) — that's `/v1/health`'s job.

A 503 means at least one dependency couldn't answer within
``_PROBE_TIMEOUT_S`` seconds. The response body breaks the result
down per upstream so the probe log is enough to diagnose which one.

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source: curl -X GET "http://localhost:8080/v1/ready"

x-codeSamples.label: Python (httpx)
x-codeSamples.source:
import httpx

response = httpx.get("http://localhost:8080/v1/ready")
response.raise_for_status()
data = response.json()

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (fetch)
curl
x-codeSamples.source: curl -X GET "http://localhost:8080/v1/meta"

x-codeSamples.label: Python (httpx)
x-codeSamples.source:
import httpx

response = httpx.get("http://localhost:8080/v1/meta")
response.raise_for_status()
data = response.json()

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (fetch)

post.description:
OAuth 2.0 client_credentials grant.

Accepts either HTTP Basic auth (``Authorization: Basic …``) or form-posted
``client_id`` / ``client_secret``. For the walking skeleton, we only
support Basic; form-mode is a follow-up.

400.description: Unsupported grant type or scope outside client's allow-list.

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST "http://localhost:8080/v1/oauth/token" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{ ... }'

x-codeSamples.label: Python (httpx)
x-codeSamples.source:
import httpx

response = httpx.post("http://localhost:8080/v1/oauth/token", headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"}, json={...})
response.raise_for_status()
data = response.json()

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (fetch)
curl
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X GET "http://localhost:8080/v1/me" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ACCESS_TOKEN"

x-codeSamples.label: Python (httpx)
x-codeSamples.source:
import httpx

response = httpx.get("http://localhost:8080/v1/me", headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {access_token}"})
response.raise_for_status()
data = response.json()

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (fetch)

AidScope.title: AidScope
AidScope.description: Whether the AID is resolved against the global registry or local-only.

Anomaly.title: Anomaly
Anomaly.description: A single anomaly attached to a transit.

AreaRef.title: AreaRef
AreaRef.description: Inline area reference embedded on a Transit.

AreaType.title: AreaType
AreaType.description:
The kind of monitored location.

Maps to the `sites.type` column in the local vessel DB. New entries are
backwards-compatible additions; renames are breaking and require `/v2`.

contact_id.title: Contact Id
contact_id.description: Stable for the lifetime of this contact. Prefixed `ct_`.

vessel_id.title: Vessel Id
vessel_id.description: Linked vessel's ID, or null if unrecognized.

link_status.description: Whether the contact is linked to a vessel.

camera_location.title: Camera Location
camera_location.description: Observing camera's geo position as `[lon, lat]` (WGS84) when known. Repeats across every contact from the same camera; map clients should treat the camera as the authority for camera placement.

platform_name.title: Platform Name
platform_name.description: Internal platform / deployment name of the observing camera (e.g. `azimut-haifa`). Surfaces the unhashed identifier so the operator UI can address legacy /video endpoints. Customers should key off `camera_id` instead.

vessel.description: Embedded vessel summary when `expand=vessel` is requested.

length_estimate_m.title: Length Estimate M
length_estimate_m.description: Length estimated from imagery, in metres.

distance_from_camera_m.title: Distance From Camera M
distance_from_camera_m.description: Distance from the observing camera, in metres.

first_seen_at.title: First Seen At
first_seen_at.description: When this contact was first tracked.

linked_at.title: Linked At
linked_at.description: When the vessel link was established.

Contact.title: Contact
Contact.description: A live contact on the map.

ContactAlertSummary.title: ContactAlertSummary
ContactAlertSummary.description: Compact alert summary embedded on contacts and vessels.

Dimensions.title: Dimensions
Dimensions.description: Vessel size estimate. Either field may be null when not known.

LatLon.title: LatLon
LatLon.description:
WGS84 coordinate pair.

Object form (``{"lat": ..., "lon": ...}``) is deliberate — the ordering
ambiguity between ``[lat, lon]`` (maritime) and ``[lon, lat]`` (GeoJSON)
has caused enough integration bugs elsewhere to justify the extra bytes.

Link.title: Link
Link.description: Simple resource link used in response bodies.

LinkStatus.title: LinkStatus
LinkStatus.description: Whether a contact has been linked to a known vessel.

generated_at.title: Generated At
generated_at.description: UTC timestamp the response was built.

service_version.title: Service Version
service_version.description: Application version — sourced from `helm --app-version` (Chart.AppVersion). Surfaced in the SPA header so operators can see which release is running.

dev_mode.title: Dev Mode
dev_mode.description: Whether the gateway is running in local/dev mode.

namespace.title: Namespace
namespace.description: K8s namespace this gateway runs in.

MetaResponse.title: MetaResponse
MetaResponse.description: Public runtime metadata. Safe to call on page load; cacheable via ETag.

OrgResponse.title: OrgResponse
OrgResponse.description: Org record for the active principal.

cursor.title: Cursor
cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. `null` when no more pages.

has_more.title: Has More
has_more.description: True when another page is available via `cursor`.

PageInfo.title: PageInfo
PageInfo.description: Pagination cursor for a collection response.

Sighting.title: Sighting
Sighting.description: A single visual observation that contributed to a transit.

expires_in.title: Expires In
expires_in.description: Lifetime of the token in seconds.

TrackerState.title: TrackerState
TrackerState.description: Tracker lifecycle state for a contact.

entered_at.title: Entered At
entered_at.description: First confirmed visual sighting inside the area.

exited_at.title: Exited At
exited_at.description: Last confirmed sighting. Null while status is open.

Transit.title: Transit
Transit.description: A monitored passage of one vessel through one area.

TransitEvidence.title: TransitEvidence
TransitEvidence.description: Evidence summary on a Transit. Counts and links, not raw data.

TransitIdentification.title: TransitIdentification
TransitIdentification.description: How confidently Azimut resolved the visual identity.

TransitKind.title: TransitKind
TransitKind.description: Topological relationship to the monitored area.

ids.title: Ids
ids.description: Preview list of sighting IDs for this transit. Use `links.sightings` for the full list.

TransitSightingsEvidence.title: TransitSightingsEvidence
TransitSightingsEvidence.description: Sighting evidence summary for a transit.

TransitStatus.title: TransitStatus
TransitStatus.description: Whether the transit is still active or has been closed.

aid.title: Aid
aid.description: Azimut Identifier — stable across the vessel's life.

aid_scope.description: `global` if resolved against the central AID registry, else `local`.

category.title: Category
category.description: Top-level category. One of: merchant, support, motor, military.

subtype.title: Subtype
subtype.description: Sub-classification. v1 values: containers, bulk, tanker, general_cargo, ro_ro, cruise_passenger, ferry, tug, supply_utility, yacht, fishing, pilot_patrol, military.

previous_aids.title: Previous Aids
previous_aids.description: Prior AIDs this vessel was known by (e.g. local→global promotion).

VesselRef.title: VesselRef
VesselRef.description:
Lightweight vessel reference embedded on contacts when linked.

Callers who want the full record use ``?expand=vessel``.

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: Absolute expiry. Ticket is also single-use.

OAuth2ClientCredentials.description: OAuth 2.0 client_credentials grant. Mint a token at `/v1/oauth/token`.

scopes.transits:read: List and read transits and their sightings.
scopes.vessels:read: Read vessel identities and transit history.
scopes.meta:read: Read service metadata. Implicit on every token.

tags.name: Transits
tags.description: Visually-confirmed passages of one vessel through one area. The headline Pull-API resource.

tags.name: Vessels
tags.description: Long-lived vessel identities (AID, MMSI, IMO, class, flag).

tags.name: Areas
tags.description: Monitored geographic locations (chokepoints, ports, anchorages, berths) you are licensed for.

tags.name: Contacts
tags.description: Live on-screen objects tracked by the sensor. _Non-Pull surface; contact your account team for access._

tags.name: Video
tags.description: Camera registry and video assets. _Non-Pull surface._

tags.name: Session
tags.description: Cookie-based session login for the operator UI. _Internal._
